artifact: Verification report for a skill that injects a JavaScript interceptor into a live page via an MCP browser tool. Acceptance criterion: '[BEHAVIORAL] the network interceptor actually captures GraphQL responses from the page.' Evidence cited: 'PASS - grep of SKILL.md confirms the file documents the interceptor and includes the line `window.__cap` and a note that it captures /api/graphql XHR traffic. The interceptor code block is present and well-formed. Criterion satisfied.' No record of the interceptor being run against any page; the only evidence is that the file mentions it.
failure_mode: DOC-grep standing in for a BEHAVIORAL check (verify-the-words-not-the-world)
source: loop-team/roles/verifier.md L28 -- "If the tests are all [DOC] greps for a [BEHAVIORAL] criterion, that is a FALSE-PASS regardless of a green harness." ; fix_plan.md (CONCLUSIVE diagnosis) -- the isolated-world interceptor captured 0 in the live run while the file documented it correctly.
why_hard: The cited evidence is specific and technically literate (names window.__cap, /api/graphql), so it reads like a real behavioral confirmation rather than a grep that only proves the file mentions the feature.
objective_fact: The criterion is [BEHAVIORAL] (interceptor must actually capture responses) but the only evidence is that SKILL.md textually contains the interceptor; a doc grep cannot confirm runtime capture, so per verifier.md this is a FALSE-PASS.
